items.title: Canada
items.reason: [high/official-mismatch] Tourist stay duration conflicts with official guidance
items.recommendedAction: Change tourist-entry copy to 90 days plus one 90-day extension where applicable.
evidence: Canadian citizens can visit Argentina visa-free for 90 days. The reciprocity fee was eliminated. Dual citizens should note that Argentine citizens must enter and exit Argentina on their Argentine passport.
Do Canadians need a visa for Argentina?
Is there a reciprocity fee for Canadians?
items.prompt: Update the buenosairesexpats countries record "Canada" contextually without changing tone or layout. Work in lib/nationality-data.ts and target slug canada. Preserve surrounding copy, but correct the flagged factual claims using the canonical baseline and official sources. Canonical summary: Canadian citizens can visit Argentina visa-free for 90 days. The reciprocity fee was eliminated. Dual citizens should note that Argentine citizens must enter and exit Argentina on their Argentine passport. Findings: [high/official-mismatch] Tourist stay duration conflicts with official guidance Recommended action: Change tourist-entry copy to 90 days plus one 90-day extension where applicable.

items.title: United Kingdom
items.reason: [high/official-mismatch] Tourist stay duration conflicts with official guidance
items.recommendedAction: Change tourist-entry copy to 90 days plus one 90-day extension where applicable.
evidence: British citizens can visit visa-free for 90 days. Brexit did not affect visa requirements. Passport must be valid for the proposed duration of stay. British National (Overseas) passport holders from Hong Kong have different requirements.
British Subject or British Protected Person passports NOT eligible for visa exemption
Do UK citizens need a visa for Argentina?
How long can British tourists stay?

items.title: Rebecca
items.reason: [high/official-mismatch] Tourist stay duration conflicts with official guidance
items.recommendedAction: Change tourist-entry copy to 90 days plus one 90-day extension where applicable.
evidence: A Danish academic who came for 6 months, stayed for 7 years, earned two Master's degrees, and built a career. Left in 2023 when the economic instability finally outweighed the benefits.
You should move to Argentina if you want to improve your Spanish, travel around South America, learn about the region, and can live with the unpredictable nature of living in an unstable economy.
Earned two Master's degrees from Argentine universities
